title: Step 1: Newton's First Law — Objects Are Lazy by Nature

frames.text:
Isaac Newton (1687) changed how we understand motion forever.

His **First Law** says:

*"An object at rest stays at rest, and an object in motion stays in motion — unless a force acts on it."*

This is called **Inertia** — the resistance to change in motion.
frames.speech: Isaac Newton in 1687 changed how we understand motion forever. His First Law says: an object at rest stays at rest, and an object in motion stays in motion — unless a force acts on it. This resistance to change is called Inertia.

title: Step 2: Newton's Second Law — Force = Mass × Acceleration

frames.text:
**Second Law**: The bigger the force, the bigger the acceleration.
The bigger the mass, the smaller the acceleration.

**F = ma**

Force (Newtons) = Mass (kg) × Acceleration (m/s²)

Push a feather vs push a truck — same force, very different results!
frames.speech: Newton's Second Law says the bigger the force, the bigger the acceleration. But the bigger the mass, the smaller the acceleration. This is captured in the formula: F equals m times a. Force in Newtons equals mass in kilograms times acceleration in meters per second squared. Push a feather versus push a truck with the same force — very different results!

title: Step 3: Newton's Third Law — Every Action Has a Reaction

followup_questions.question: Why do rockets work in the vacuum of space?
followup_questions.speech: Why do rockets work even in the vacuum of space where there's nothing to push against?

followup_questions.question: What is the difference between mass and weight?
followup_questions.speech: What is the actual difference between mass and weight? Are they the same thing?

followup_questions.question: What is friction and how does it relate to Newton's laws?
followup_questions.speech: What is friction and how does it relate to Newton's laws of motion?

frames.text:
**Third Law**: For every action there is an equal and opposite reaction.

🚀 Rocket exhaust pushes down → rocket goes up
🏊 Swimmer pushes water back → swimmer moves forward
👣 You push the ground down → ground pushes you forward

Forces always come in pairs — you can't have one without the other!
frames.speech: Newton's Third Law: for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. A rocket exhaust pushes down, so the rocket goes up. A swimmer pushes water back, so the swimmer moves forward. You push the ground down when you walk, and the ground pushes you forward. Forces always come in pairs — you can never have one without the other.

frames.text:
Newton's 3 Laws in one line each:

1️⃣ **Inertia** — Things don't change unless forced to
2️⃣ **F = ma** — Force creates acceleration, mass resists it
3️⃣ **Action-Reaction** — Forces always come in pairs

These 3 laws govern everything from bouncing balls to spacecraft trajectories! 🛸
frames.speech: Here's Newton's three laws in one line each. First: Inertia — things don't change unless forced to. Second: F equals ma — force creates acceleration, mass resists it. Third: Action-Reaction — forces always come in pairs. These three laws govern everything from bouncing balls to spacecraft trajectories.
